article_title: FDA Issues Draft Guidance Letting Gene-Editing Developers Reuse Platform Data Across Products to Speed Submissions
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.message: raps.org is a reputable specialist outlet (Regulatory Affairs Professionals Society); allowlist warning is administrative, not a credibility concern. No action beyond noting; the outlet is the only one of the three providing the PDUFA VII / AAV-nanoparticle extension detail.
findings.details: Recommend adding raps.org to config/source_allowlist.txt in a separate maintenance change.

findings.message: Primary guidance document title is rendered with one word dropped versus the official Federal Register / FDA.gov title.
findings.details: Article Overview states the document is titled 'Leveraging Prior Knowledge in Development of Human Gene Therapy Products Incorporating Genome Editing'. The official title (Federal Register doc 2026-11054; FDA guidance page) is 'Leveraging Prior Knowledge in THE Development of Human Gene Therapy Products Incorporating Genome Editing' — the article omits the word 'the'. The full title does not appear verbatim in any of the three cited snapshots (which describe the guidance's content but never give its exact title); independently confirmed against the Federal Register. Title is otherwise correct and the substance is fully sourced. Recoverable via a clarification note.

content_preview.title: FDA Issues Draft Guidance Letting Gene-Editing Developers Reuse Platform Data Across Products to Speed Submissions
content_preview.summary: The FDA's June 2 draft guidance lets genome-editing sponsors leverage prior CMC, nonclinical, and clinical knowledge across products, with a 90-day comment window.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on June 2 issued draft guidance intended to help developers bring gene therapies to patients more efficiently by making greater use of existing scientific and regulatory knowledge, according to [GlobeNewswire](https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/06/02/3305422/0/en/FDA-Issues-Draft-Guidance-to-Help-Accelerate-Cell-and-Gene-Therapies-for-Patients.html). The document, titled "Leveraging Prior Knowledge in Development of Human Gene Thera...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (731 words, within the 400-1200 News range). Clean Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis arc. Technical depth is appropriate and accurate (CMC, nonclinical, clinical prior knowledge; ex vivo / in vivo somatic-cell genome editing; PDUFA VII commitment). The 'What We Don't Know' section is appropriately candid about the draft's non-binding status and unresolved scope.
editor_notes.source_verification: All three source snapshots read from disk and verified (no archive fallback; all HTTP 200). source-0.html.gz (BioSpace) and source-1.html.gz (GlobeNewswire) carry the FDA press release verbatim; source-2.html.gz (RAPS) carries the independent regulatory analysis. VERIFIED: (1) Mikhail quote 'accelerating innovation without compromising the rigorous scientific standards that patients and the public depend on' — verbatim in GlobeNewswire, attributed to Karim Mikhail, Acting Director, CBER (article matches). (2) Kumar quote 'leveraging prior knowledge does not mean lowering the bar; it means raising our collective efficiency' — verbatim in GlobeNewswire, attributed to Vijay Kumar, Acting Director, Office of Therapeutic Products in CBER (article matches). (3) Gavin quote 'We look forward to working closely with sponsors to help them understand how to effectively implement this guidance' — verbatim in BioSpace, attributed to Denise Gavin, Office of Gene Therapy — CMC (article matches; article shortens her title to 'Director of the Office of Gene Therapy — CMC', source says 'Director of the Office of Therapeutic Products' Office of Gene Therapy — CMC' — a faithful abbreviation, not a misattribution). (4) '90 days from the guidance's publication in the Federal Register' — verbatim in GlobeNewswire ('Comments must be submitted within 90 days of publication in the Federal Register at Regulations.gov'). (5) Companion guidance 'Safety Assessment of Genome Editing in Human Gene Therapy Products Using Next-Generation Sequencing' and 'Plausible Mechanism Framework' — both verbatim in BioSpace. (6) PDUFA VII commitment and the AAV / nanoparticle / ex-vivo-cell extension language — verbatim in RAPS. (7) The 'submit leveraging proposals to the FDA ... provide a justification for the applicability of the data' claim — supported by RAPS ('Sponsors should submit any leveraging proposals to FDA for consideration ... provide a scientific rationale demonstrating the applicability of the data being leveraged').
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Headline, summary, and Overview lead are all sourced (June 2 issuance, leveraging prior CMC/nonclinical/clinical knowledge, 90-day window — all in GlobeNewswire/BioSpace). The bot correctly did NOT invent a docket number or a specific calendar comment deadline; it used the verifiable 'within 90 days of Federal Register publication' framing exactly as the press release states. ONE imprecision: the primary guidance document's exact title is rendered as 'Leveraging Prior Knowledge in Development of Human Gene Therapy Products Incorporating Genome Editing', dropping the word 'the' from the official 'Leveraging Prior Knowledge in THE Development of...' (Federal Register doc 2026-11054). The full title does not appear verbatim in any cited snapshot; the title is real and otherwise correct (independently confirmed against the Federal Register). Recoverable via a clarification note.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference. The headline verb 'Letting ... Reuse Platform Data' is descriptive and accurate, not editorializing. The Analysis section stays analytical and grounded in sourced facts.
editor_notes.originality: New topic. No overlap with the two other FDA-tagged June 2026 articles (Replimune oncolytic virus; Xocova COVID drug). The internal link to the Feb 2026 Plausible Mechanism Pathway article is valid (target file exists) and is a genuine, relevant prior-coverage connection rather than a forced link.
concerns: Primary guidance title omits the word 'the' relative to the official Federal Register / FDA.gov title; full title not present verbatim in any cited snapshot (filed as a clarification on merge).
raps.org is not yet in config/source_allowlist.txt — administrative warning only; the outlet is reputable (Regulatory Affairs Professionals Society).
recommendations: Add raps.org to config/source_allowlist.txt in a separate maintenance commit.
File the document-title clarification as a corrections record on merge.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Substantively accurate, well-sourced News piece with all three official quotes verified verbatim and the comment-window framing correctly restrained (no fabricated docket or deadline). The only issue is a one-word omission in the primary guidance's official title, which is real and otherwise correct. This is honestly summarizable in a single clarification note, so APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS rather than REJECT (the headline/summary/lead are all independently sound).
